title: review-loop-duo finding output
description: JSON Schema for a single reviewer's structured output. Both Claude side and Codex side emit this format. OpenAI strict mode compliant.

perspective.description: Top-level perspective tag. Per-Agent invocations (Claude side) set the single perspective they covered. Unified multi-perspective invocations (Codex side) set 'multi' and rely on per-finding `perspective` to identify each finding's category.

id.description: Locally unique id within this output (e.g. CR-1, SE-3)

perspective.description: Per-finding perspective tag. REQUIRED so multi-perspective invocations (top-level perspective='multi') can carry findings from all 8 perspectives in a single response. Single-perspective invocations should set this to match the top-level perspective.

line.description: Line number. null when no specific line (file-level finding)

category.description: Short slug for dedup (e.g. tenant-isolation, n-plus-one, empty-catch, lossy-fallback-before-side-effect)

why_problem.description: なぜ問題なのか（機序）. The MOST IMPORTANT field. Explain WHAT the code does and WHICH invariant/precondition/contract/intent it breaks. Describe the mechanism of the defect, not just its name. Must NOT be the same text as impact.

impact.description: なぜ修正が必要なのか（帰結）. The concrete harm if left unfixed: who is affected, in what situation, and what is lost (data corruption / leak / outage / wrong behavior / maintenance cost). This justifies the severity. Must NOT restate why_problem.

fix.description: どう修正すると良いか（方針）. The recommended fix direction in prose. Concrete code goes in before/after.

rule_ref.description: Reference to a coding rule doc, e.g. docs/coding-rules/backend/security.md#L45
